intent: keep hacking past 23:00 Stockholm night after night on side projects (whisper-writer streaming, jhana voice loop, ghostty/hook tweaks, intender polish)
finding: Recurring pattern of substantive user activity past 23:00 Stockholm across at least eight distinct evenings in the review window (2026-03-22, 04-04, 04-07, 04-09, 04-10, 04-11, 04-14, 04-15, 04-17). Not a single late session — it's a rhythm. Quantified: Apr 11 had 56 user turns past 23:00 across 4+ parallel sessions; Apr 14-15 spans 22:20 to 00:14 (41 turns in whisper-writer) with a fresh session started at 00:15 (12 more late-night turns); Apr 7 23:10-00:00 had 44 turns across 5 sessions tweaking hooks and ghostty config; Apr 17 22:13-23:01 ended with the user handing off an overnight autonomous run. Late-night turns tend to cluster on infrastructure tweaks, harness work, and voice/hook plumbing rather than urgent deadlines — matches the 'hyperfocused but unsustainable' signature in categories.md. Existing wellbeing-nudge.py hook is the right shape of mitigation (late-night threshold 20:00+5h with 60-min cooldown) — this observation flags that the rate still warrants attention, not that the hook is broken.
existing_mitigation: /home/jonathan/.claude/wellbeing-nudge.py — UserPromptSubmit hook nudges after 20:00 when >=5h active today, with a 60-min cooldown and breathwork reminder; late-marathon (>=8h any time) and continuous-block (>=1.5h unbroken) triggers also present. Hook fires; pattern persists despite it.

intent: hand Claude a sprawling autonomous mandate at the end of the day and go to sleep ('while I sleep', 'don't return control', 'at least 3 hours')
finding: On 2026-04-17 between 22:20 and 23:01 Stockholm the user explicitly framed an overnight autonomous run in the intender-app session: 'I want you to be an autonomous agent while I sleep', 'happy autonomous night', 'whatever you do, don't return control until [beautiful]', 'keep at it for a long long time, until it's polished. I'm expecting at least 3 hours.' Scope also escalated mid-setup — an initial 'make the app beautiful' picked up additional asks (permission-onboarding research in subagent, design adaptation notes) in the same 40-minute window. This matches the manic-mode signatures in categories.md: late-evening start, scope escalation, abrupt handoff. Rating medium rather than high: only one clear instance in the window, but it's qualitatively different from plain late-night work (OBS-001) — the risk is not only the user's bedtime but a commitment to a multi-hour unsupervised run whose output has to be triaged the next morning. Worth surfacing as its own shape; may recur.
existing_mitigation: None found. wellbeing-nudge.py catches the late-hour at prompt-submit time but has no specific detection or friction for 'run autonomously overnight' framing; step-back-nudge/detector address a different axis (stuck loops, not volitional overextension).

intent: juggle several projects in parallel late-evening sessions on the same night (weekend repo + jhana voice + global .claude tweaks + personal-assistant skeleton, all inside a 3-hour window after 20:00)
finding: On 2026-04-11 evening (20:16 Stockholm onward) the user opened 18 distinct user-driven sessions across 5+ projects (weekend, jhana, claude, intender-adjacent) with heavy overlap: multiple sessions running concurrently, short 0-2 minute 'drive-by' sessions interleaved with longer ones, and four very late sessions started 23:30-23:47 whose next user turn did not arrive until the following morning (08:36+) — consistent with stopping mid-flow rather than a deliberate close. Short hand-off fragments ('I want you to commit changes', 'I want the claude override w color to clear before claude') suggest cognitive-load shedding. This is a multi-session-in-short-window signature (manic-mode indicator per categories.md) distinct from the plain late-hours shape in OBS-001. Rating medium: single clear day in the window with this specific 'parallel-project thrash' shape, but it co-occurred with OBS-001's late-night pattern, so a reviewer may reasonably cluster them.
existing_mitigation: wellbeing-nudge.py computes 'active time today' across all sessions, so late-evening multi-session does contribute to the 5h/8h thresholds; no specific detector for parallel-session fan-out or project-switching intensity.
